new_value: Delivered in PR #150 (universal agent prompt template) and PR #177 (Codex integration). Template lives at docs/integrations/agent-prompt-template.md.
3/4 subtasks delivered: toy walkthrough (#147), universal agent template (#150/#177), embedded LLM (yzma). Remaining git hooks (ne7.3) is standalone.
Core portability story shipped: skill packs, remote sources (#179), pack curation (#182), seed generation (#169). Remaining items are independent future features.
Cross-store edge routing fixed (#143), scope classifier fixed in nuclear relearn (#114), edge JSONL sync fixed (#163). Core issues resolved.
Language field added to FloopActiveInput and wired through handler_active (PR #183)
--language flag added to CLI floop learn command (PR #183)
Both values already 0.3; removed stale docs footnote (PR #183)
Simulation test for ancient edge temporal decay boundary (PR #184)
Simulation test for feature affinity virtual edges (PR #184)
Simulation test for conflict edge propagation with inhibition (PR #184)
Simulation test for bounds invariants (100-session stress test, PR #184)
README restructured with why section, simplified quickstart, progressive disclosure (PR #185)
FALSE_POSITIVE: TestSQLiteGraphStore_SyncReconcilesTruncatedJSONL already exists and tests truncation recovery. Coverage is thin but the claim was overstated.
FALSE_POSITIVE: Losers are always below winners by construction (sorted descending, losers are nodes[m:]). meanWinnerAct >= any loser activation. Floor check also exists.
FALSE_POSITIVE: Store layer validates and rejects negative edge weights (sqlite_test.go:1005-1013 confirms wantErr:true for weight:-0.5). Engine can never encounter negative weights from the store.
FALSE_POSITIVE: Tags are deduplicated at extraction time. extract.go:18 says 'sorted, deduplicated, sanitized'. merge.go:21 says 'sorted, deduplicated'. All tag sources go through these functions.

DESIGN_CHOICE: Bidirectional traversal is intentional and tested (TestEngine_BidirectionalPropagation). Spreading activation in semantic graph is undirected by design. The real issue is floop-pzg (overrides semantics).

DESIGN_CHOICE: Max-aggregation is documented and intentional for stability. Code comment says 'use max to prevent runaway activation'. TestEngine_FanIn documents: 'A should get max of incoming, not sum'.

DESIGN_CHOICE: d>=1.0 is mathematically invalid (integral diverges). Sentinel return -10.0 maps to ~0.0001 after sigmoid — effectively zero. Default config hardcodes d=0.5. Not user-configurable at runtime.

DESIGN_CHOICE: Intentional and documented. constants.go:41-42 explicitly states weights are proportional. Redistribution approach is mathematically sound for optional signals. Scores only compared within same pipeline run.
DESIGN_CHOICE: 64-bit hash at ~100s behaviors has negligible collision risk (50% at ~4B). Intentional truncation for shorter DB values. Could add documenting comment but not a code fix.

{"description":"CONFIRMED: Traverse acquires RLock at sqlite.go:874, calls traverseRecursive which calls GetEdges at sqlite.go:799 which tries RLock again. Go's sync.RWMutex is NOT re-entrant — deadlocks under writer contention. Fix: add getEdgesUnlocked variant (analogous to existing getNodeUnlocked). Severity: HIGH."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-e0f","title":"Deadlock in SQLiteGraphStore.Traverse — re-entrant RLock","description":"Traverse acquires RLock, calls traverseRecursive which calls GetEdges which tries RLock again. Under writer contention this deadlocks. Fix: add getEdgesUnlocked variant. Location: sqlite.go:874-898","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:51:25Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:51:25Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED: propagateStep only special-cases EdgeKindConflicts. All other edge kinds (overrides, deprecated-to, merged-into) fall into the else branch and spread positive activation. Production data has 65 overrides edges — seeding A where A overrides B actively promotes B. Fix: treat overrides/deprecated/merged as suppressive or non-propagating. Severity: MEDIUM-HIGH."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-pzg","title":"Overrides/deprecated-to/merged-into edges spread positive activation","description":"Only conflicts edges are special-cased in spreading engine. An overrides edge A→B spreads positive energy to the overridden behavior B, effectively resurrecting it. These directional edge kinds should either suppress or not propagate. Location: engine.go:148-159","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:52:19Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:52:19Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED (IN PRODUCTION): predicateMatches at scorer.go:185-201 only checks ctx.FilePath \\!= '' etc — pure presence checks. Never compares behavior When values against context field values. A behavior scoped to language:python matches equally when editing Go files. Either a real bug or deliberate simplification where activation handles specificity elsewhere. Severity: MEDIUM."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-dge","title":"contextScore checks key presence not value matching","description":"scorer.go:183-202 predicateMatches checks whether context has a non-empty value for the key, not whether the behavior When value matches. A behavior scoped to language:python gets the same context score viewing Go file as Python file. Actual value matching happens in activation filtering, so this signal is context completeness not context match.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:53:13Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:53:13Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED (POTENTIAL PANIC): ValuesEqual at util.go:6-44 has branches for []interface{}+[]interface{} and []string+[]string, but mixed types (JSON deserialization produces []interface{}, Go code produces []string) fall through to 'return a == b' which PANICS for slices in Go. No test coverage for mixed types. Fix: add mixed-type conversion branch. Severity: MEDIUM."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-2dq","title":"ValuesEqual fails for mixed []string / []interface{} comparison","description":"similarity/util.go:6-44 — JSON unmarshaling produces []interface{} while Go code produces []string. Fall-through to interface equality always returns false for semantically identical slices. No test for this case.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:53:46Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:53:46Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED (LOW SEVERITY): addBehavior at sqlite.go:175-397 does 3 separate ExecContext calls (INSERT behavior, INSERT when, INSERT stats) without a transaction. App-level mutex prevents concurrency issues within process, but crash between inserts leaves orphan rows. Real atomicity gap, low practical impact."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-d3w","title":"Non-atomic addBehavior — no transaction wrapping INSERT behavior + when + stats","description":"addBehavior executes INSERT behavior, INSERT when-conditions, INSERT stats as separate statements without a transaction. Crash between them leaves inconsistent state. Same for DeleteNode (DELETE behavior + DELETE edges). Location: sqlite.go:175-397, 660-675","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:51:28Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:51:28Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED (LOW SEVERITY): os.Create directly to target file at sqlite.go:1051, no temp+rename, no fsync. Mitigated by SQLite being primary store and reconcileNodeCount detecting mismatches. JSONL is best-effort export, not durability mechanism."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-age","title":"JSONL sync not crash-safe — no write-to-temp-then-rename, no fsync","description":"Sync writes JSONL via os.Create without write-to-temp-then-rename pattern. No fsync before close. Crash during write truncates/corrupts the file. Location: sqlite.go:935-980, 1051-1065","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:51:35Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:51:35Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED: outDegree at engine.go:138 counts ALL edges including conflict edges. Adding conflicts dilutes energy through positive edges. Example: 3 requires + 2 conflict = outDegree 5, so requires edges get 1/5 instead of 1/3. Fix: count only non-conflict edges in denominator. Severity: MEDIUM."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-d2v","title":"Conflict edges inflate outDegree, diluting positive energy spread","description":"outDegree denominator at engine.go:138 includes conflict edges that don't spread positive energy. More conflicts = weaker positive spread. A well-connected conflict hub spreads almost no positive energy through its real edges.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:52:25Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:52:25Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED: Virtual affinity edges appended to edges slice at engine.go:128-132 BEFORE outDegree computed at line 138. If node has 3 real edges and 10 virtual affinity edges, outDegree=13, diluting real edge energy to 1/13. Same class as floop-d2v. Fix: separate outDegree calculation for real vs virtual edges. Severity: MEDIUM."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-g30","title":"Virtual affinity edges inflate outDegree unpredictably","description":"engine.go:127-132,138 — Virtual affinity edges appended to real edges before outDegree computed. Behaviors with common tags have real-edge energy diluted by affinity edge count in denominator.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:26Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:26Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED: readNodesFromJSONL at sqlite.go:1100-1111 swallows decode errors with 'continue', no logging. Silent data loss. Compare with ImportNodesFromJSONL which prints to stderr. Fix: at minimum log.Printf the error."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-925","title":"readNodesFromJSONL silently skips malformed lines","description":"sqlite.go:1100-1111 — Decode errors are swallowed with continue, no logging. Corruption goes unnoticed. Compare with ImportNodesFromJSONL which at least prints to stderr.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:02Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:02Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED: Only InMemoryGraphStore has a concurrent test (memory_test.go:287). Zero concurrent tests for SQLiteGraphStore or MultiGraphStore. No t.Parallel(), no sync.WaitGroup usage. Given floop-e0f deadlock, these tests would catch it."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-n3t","title":"No concurrent access tests for store layer","description":"No tests exercise concurrent read/write, the Traverse deadlock scenario, or concurrent Sync + AddNode. Should test RecordActivationHit from multiple goroutines.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:12Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:12Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED: Breadth=0 at inhibition.go:78 causes meanWinnerAct = 0/0 = NaN. Only reachable via custom config (default is 7). Fix: validate Breadth \u003e 0 in NewEngine or ApplyInhibition."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-96h","title":"InhibitionConfig Breadth=0 causes division by zero / NaN propagation","description":"inhibition.go:78 — Division by zero when breadth is zero. meanWinnerAct = 0/0 = NaN. Every loser gets NaN suppression. Should validate at construction.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:53:19Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:53:19Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED: NewEngine at engine.go:90 has zero validation. DecayFactor\u003e1 causes amplification, MinActivation\u003c0 disables filtering, SpreadFactor=0 zeros energy, Breadth=0 NaNs. DefaultConfig is safe but nothing prevents bad custom configs."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-gfb","title":"No config validation in NewEngine — bad params silently degrade","description":"engine.go:90 — No validation: DecayFactor \u003e 1 causes amplification, MinActivation \u003c 0 disables filtering, SpreadFactor \u003c= 0 produces zero energy. Silent misconfiguration.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:53:22Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:53:22Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED (LOW — NOT IN PRODUCTION): ExponentialDecay at decay.go:12-28 has no halfLife guard. Zero→Inf→score 0. Negative→growth\u003e1.0. However this function is NEVER called in production code — only in tests. Production uses EdgeDecay. Consider removing or guarding."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-6nh","title":"ExponentialDecay has no bounds on halfLife — zero/negative produce Inf/growth","description":"Zero halfLife produces Inf lambda → silent 0.0 return. Negative halfLife produces scores \u003e 1.0 (exponential growth). EdgeDecay similarly unguarded for negative weight/rho. Location: decay.go:22-27, 38-52","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:52:28Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:52:28Z"}

new_value: {"description":"DESIGN_CHOICE (NOT DEADLOCK): Lock order is consistent (m.mu → s.mu), so no deadlock from ordering. But m.mu is redundant — underlying stores already serialize themselves. Causes unnecessary serialization. Not a correctness bug, just overhead.","priority":3}
old_value: {"id":"floop-hf4","title":"MultiGraphStore double-locking — redundant outer mutex with TOCTOU gaps","description":"MultiGraphStore holds its own m.mu lock, then delegates to SQLiteGraphStore methods that acquire their own s.mu lock. The outer lock provides no real atomicity since each delegate call acquires/releases independently. TOCTOU gaps between check-and-act calls. Location: multi.go:65-452","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:51:35Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:51:35Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED (MINOR): SetMaxOpenConns(25) at sqlite.go:59. SQLite only allows 1 writer at a time. 25 conns wastes resources, increases SQLITE_BUSY risk. 2-4 appropriate with WAL mode. Not a correctness bug."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-t53","title":"SQLite MaxOpenConns(25) is excessive","description":"sqlite.go:59 — 25 connections is excessive for file-based SQLite. With WAL mode and app-level mutex, 2-4 is appropriate. Reduces risk of SQLITE_BUSY errors.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:00Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:00Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED: No test compares Activate and ActivateWithSteps results on same graph. Divergence already exists — ActivateWithSteps passes nil for distance/seedSource at engine.go:219. A cross-validation test would catch any drift between the two code paths."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-jz3","title":"No cross-validation test between Activate and ActivateWithSteps","description":"ActivateWithSteps builds activation independently without distance/seedSource. No test verifies final snapshot matches Activate results for same graph and seeds.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:18Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:18Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED (LOW — NOT IN PRODUCTION): NewHybridScorer at hybrid.go:39-48 does no weight validation/normalization. However HybridScorer has ZERO production callers — only in hybrid.go and hybrid_test.go. Forward-looking infrastructure not yet wired in."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-p4n","title":"HybridScorer weights not validated or normalized","description":"hybrid.go:39-48 — Unlike RelevanceScorer which normalizes, HybridScorer accepts weights as-is. Custom configs can produce scores \u003e 1.0 if weights sum \u003e 1.0.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:28Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:28Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED (LOW — DEPRECATED): BoostTracker.boostTimes map grows without eviction. BUT ApplyReinforcement is explicitly marked Deprecated (line 89), superseded by ACT-R. Zero production callers. Consider removing entirely."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-yfh","title":"BoostTracker entries accumulate unboundedly","description":"reinforcement.go:64-87 — AllowBoost filters old timestamps for queried behavior, but behaviors never queried again retain entries forever. No periodic cleanup or eviction.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:30Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:30Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED (LOW — NOT IN PRODUCTION): Traverse delegates to single store, no cross-store traversal. Cross-store edges exist (AddEdge stores them) but Traverse won't follow them. However Traverse has zero production callers — activation pipeline uses GetEdges which does merge across stores."}
old_value: {"id":"floop-57j","title":"MultiGraphStore.Traverse doesn't cross store boundaries","description":"multi.go:283-306 — Traversal confined to single store. Edges between local and global stores not followed. Comment says 'delegates to local store only for simplicity' but silently produces incomplete results.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"51134175+nvandessel@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:33Z","created_by":"Nic van Dessel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T01:54:33Z"}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED: Traverse acquires RLock at sqlite.go:874, calls traverseRecursive which calls GetEdges at sqlite.go:799 which tries RLock again. Go's sync.RWMutex is NOT re-entrant — deadlocks under writer contention. Fix: add getEdgesUnlocked variant (analogous to existing getNodeUnlocked). Severity: HIGH."}

new_value: {"description":"CONFIRMED: propagateStep only special-cases EdgeKindConflicts. All other edge kinds (overrides, deprecated-to, merged-into) fall into the else branch and spread positive activation. Production data has 65 overrides edges — seeding A where A overrides B actively promotes B. Fix: treat overrides/deprecated/merged as suppressive or non-propagating. Severity: MEDIUM-HIGH."}
